title: EDOT Collector Configuration Guide
body:
# EDOT Collector Configuration

The EDOT Collector supports both Agent and Gateway deployment modes.

## Receivers

The OTLP receiver accepts traces, metrics, and logs via gRPC (port 4317) and HTTP (port 4318).

```yaml
receivers:
  otlp:
    protocols:
      grpc:
        endpoint: "0.0.0.0:4317"
      http:
        endpoint: "0.0.0.0:4318"
```

## Processors

Always use memory_limiter as the first processor in any pipeline.

```yaml
processors:
  memory_limiter:
    check_interval: 1s
    limit_mib: 1000
  batch:
    send_batch_size: 10000
    timeout: 10s
```

title: EDOT Python SDK Quick Start
body:
# EDOT Python SDK

Install the EDOT Python SDK:

```bash
pip install elastic-opentelemetry
```

Auto-instrumentation:

```bash
opentelemetry-instrument python your_app.py
```

The EDOT Python SDK includes pre-configured exporters for Elastic APM.

title: OpenTelemetry Collector Architecture
body:
# Collector Architecture

The OpenTelemetry Collector has three main components:

1. **Receivers** - Accept telemetry data from sources
2. **Processors** - Transform and enrich data
3. **Exporters** - Send data to backends

Data flows through pipelines: receivers -> processors -> exporters.

title: elastic/opentelemetry v1.2.0 Release
body:
# v1.2.0

Release highlights:
- Added support for new processors
- Updated to upstream OTel Collector v0.95.0
- Bug fixes for memory limiter

title: Getting Started with EDOT on Kubernetes
body:
# EDOT on Kubernetes

Deploy the EDOT Collector as a DaemonSet for per-node collection, or as a Deployment for gateway mode.

## DaemonSet (Agent Mode)

Collects host metrics and local telemetry on each node.

## Deployment (Gateway Mode)

Centralized processing with tail sampling and load balancing.
